message.content:
CRITICAL: Verify changes made to InvoiceCashbackOnboarding.

The progress.txt states multiple fixes:
1) "React key warning - changed from undefined item.text to index-based key for skeleton map"
2) "Removed unused props (pts, price, pointsQuantity, conversionFactor) from InvoiceCashbackViewProps interface and component call"
3) "Removed unused variables (finalPrice, pointsPrice, points)"

I need you to:
1. Read src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx
2. Read src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/view.tsx
3. Check if the removed props/variables were TRULY unused or if they were passed to child components
4. Check if the key change affects list rendering behavior

Report if these were SAFE cleanups or BEHAVIOR-CHANGING modifications.

content.text: I'll verify the changes made to InvoiceCashbackOnboarding by examining both the index and view files.

content.content:
1→import React, { FC } from 'react';
     2→import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
     3→import { Box, Text, ListTextIcon } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
     4→import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
     5→import { Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
     6→import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';
     7→
     8→const InvoiceCashbackView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
     9→  items,
    10→  hasError,
    11→  hasLoading,
    12→  handleGoBack,
    13→  handleContinue,
    14→  handleRetry,
    15→  cashbackInvoiceList,
    16→  isLoadingParameterization,
    17→  width,
    18→}) => {
    19→  return (
    20→    <BasicScreen
    21→      hasScroll
    22→      hasHeader
    23→      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
    24→      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
    25→      title="Cashback na fatura"
    26→      hasRightIcon={false}
    27→      errorButtonOnPress={handleRetry}
    28→      isLoading={hasLoading}
    29→      hasError={hasError}
    30→    >
    31→      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
    32→        <Text
    33→          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
    34→          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
    35→          type="headingMDL"
    36→          mt="smaller"
    37→        >
    38→          Use pontos para pagar menos na fatura
    39→        </Text>
    40→      </Box>
    41→      {isLoadingParameterization &&
    42→        cashbackInvoiceList?.map?.((item, index) => (
    43→          <Box pd="innerLarge" key={`skeleton-${index}`}>
    44→            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
    45→          </Box>
    46→        ))}
    47→      {!isLoadingParameterization && (
    48→        <>
    49→          <Box ml="nano" mr="nano" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
    50→            <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="xs" mt="xxs" type="list" />
    51→          </Box>
    52→
    53→          <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
    54→            <Button
    55→              text="Continuar"
    56→              mt="large"
    57→              mb="small"
    58→              onPress={handleContinue}
    59→              testID="invoice-cashback-button"
    60→            />
    61→          </Box>
    62→        </>
    63→      )}
    64→    </BasicScreen>
    65→  );
    66→};
    67→
    68→export default InvoiceCashbackView;
    69→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
